steps.title: Identify the decisive clue
steps.content: The phrase "without looking at the rest of us" is key. It shows that Esteban didn't pause, turn, or acknowledge anyone—he moved straight ahead on a single path.

steps.title: Apply the meaning
steps.content:
Substitute each choice for "straight" in: "He walked [word] to his seat without looking at the rest of us."

A. Delightedly means with delight or happiness. Esteban came in with his head down and dripping wet—the scene conveys no joy. This reading contradicts the passage. Eliminated.

B. Truthfully means in an honest manner. You don't walk truthfully to a seat; this meaning doesn't fit physical movement. Eliminated.

C. Directly means in a straight line, without detouring or stopping. "He walked directly to his seat" matches the image of Esteban moving without pausing or acknowledging anyone. Correct.

D. Formally means in a stiff or ceremonious way. Nothing in the passage suggests ceremonious behavior—he's soaking wet and keeping his head down. Eliminated.

C is correct.
